question.content: In a pedigree problem that combines beta-thalassemia and an X-linked fava-bean disease (female carriers are phenotypically normal), the local pathogenic-allele frequency is 10%. Individual II-4 is a normal male and marries a phenotypically normal female. Find the probability that their child is a boy with fava-bean disease. Answer format: one final result only. If the result naturally simplifies to a single value, give that value; otherwise give a single algebraic expression using the symbols defined in the problem. Use ASCII characters only. Do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign. Answer format: a simplest fraction such as a/b; write only the fraction itself, with no explanatory text, units, or equals sign.

step_1.content: Identify II-4 as a normal male in an X-linked fava-bean pedigree.
step_1.key_point: A son gets Y from father and X from mother.

step_3.content: For II-4 (normal male), only maternal mutant X can produce an affected son.
step_3.key_point: P(affected boy) = P(boy) * P(maternal mutant X transmission).

step_5.content: Use the local mutant-allele frequency q = 0.1 for the mother side.
step_5.key_point: Combine q with the boy probability 1/2.

step_7.content: Multiply the two factors and keep only one compact final expression.
